title: Anatomy of a Metric Space
description: A visual breakdown of the core components required to define a metric space and examples illustrating the concept.
elements: Axioms: d(x,y) ≥ 0, d(x,y)=0 iff x=y, d(x,y)=d(y,x), Triangle Inequality d(x,z) ≤ d(x,y) + d(y,z)
relationships: The Metric d is defined on the Cartesian product X × X
The Axioms govern the behavior of the Metric d for all points in the Set X
Together, the Set and the Metric satisfying the Axioms form the Metric Space (X,d)
